question_text: Which statement correctly describes the relationship between gas pressure and temperature?

options.label: A
options.text: Pressure and temperature are inversely proportional

options.label: B
options.text: Pressure and temperature are directly proportional

options.label: C
options.text: Pressure has no connection to temperature

correct_answer: Pressure and temperature are directly proportional
explanation: For a fixed volume of gas, when you heat it, the gas particles move faster and collide with the container walls more frequently and forcefully, increasing pressure. This direct proportional relationship is expressed as P ∝ T (at constant volume), meaning if you double the absolute temperature, the pressure doubles too.
